mappings.rationaleAI: Law covering civil law, criminal law, and major legal branches broadly addresses property law (A), family law (B), economic transactions (D), and civil procedure (E).

Legal frameworks governing family relationships directly address family law (B). Marriage, civil partnership, and divorce address the marriage contract (B.1.a) and termination of marriage (B.1.c). Children's rights and parental definitions address laws concerning children (B.1.b).

Intellectual property law covering copyright, patents, and trademarks directly addresses laws protecting intangible property rights (A.5), including rights to artistic works (A.5.b), inventions (A.5.c), and symbols identifying business products (A.5.d).
Employment rights and labor laws governing the employment relationship directly address labour law (D.4).
Competition law covering cartels, monopoly power, and merger regulation addresses law of commercial transactions (D.2) and law of business associations (D.3) through market regulation frameworks.
Comparative law examining legal families including common law and civil law addresses distinctions in property law traditions (A) and economic transaction frameworks (D) across jurisdictions.

American legal history covering property rights expansion addresses historical development of property rights (A.1). The rise of tort law and negligence after the Civil War addresses the law of torts (C) and the doctrine of strict liability versus negligence (C.1).
The American judicial system covering courts, jurisdiction, and judicial processes addresses civil procedural law (E) and elements of civil procedure including jurisdiction and venue (E.1).
The jury system covering civil juries and juror selection addresses civil procedural law (E), definitions of parties to a suit (E.1.c), and the conduct of civil trials and the law of evidence (E.2).
Arbitration as dispute resolution outside traditional courts addresses civil procedural law (E). Commercial dispute resolution bridges private law and commercial transactions (D.2).
Negotiation theory covering mediation, dispute resolution, and legal negotiation contexts addresses civil procedural law (E) as an alternative to formal litigation.

Medical law covering medical negligence and best interests determinations addresses the law of torts (C) and injuries resulting from negligent acts including failure to comply with standards of care (C.4).

Privacy covering media intrusion, data protection, and surveillance addresses injuries to personality and personal relationships (C.5) including defamation, invasions of privacy, and interference with personal relationships (C.5.a).
International law covering WTO disputes and transnational legal enforcement addresses jurisdiction in private international law and the conflict of laws (E.1.b).
Accounting principles covering financial statements, auditing, and corporate governance address the law of business associations (D.3) and the structure of corporate finance (D.3.c).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationaleAI: Banking systems covering capital allocation and financial regulation address the law of commercial transactions (D.2) and business associations (D.3).
Mathematical finance covering asset valuation, risk management, and financial contracts addresses commercial transactions (D.2) and corporate finance structure (D.3.c).
Microeconomics covering market structures, monopoly, competition, and antitrust regulation provides economic context for laws governing economic transactions (D).
Taxation covering income and capital gains tax affects economic transactions and the distribution of wealth relevant to laws governing economic transactions (D).

Polygamy covering plural marriage as an institution across cultures and its intersection with Christianity, Islam, and Mormonism addresses family law (B) and laws concerning the marriage contract (B.1.a).
The rule of law covering due process and legal constraints on power provides foundational principles for civil procedural law (E).
